description: Thales is reported by later ancient sources to have visited Egypt, where he encountered Egyptian geometry and used it to calculate the height of the pyramids by their shadows — a method demonstrating knowledge of similar triangles. Herodotus credits Egyptian land surveying with inspiring Greek geometry. The influence is real but less direct than the Babylonian astronomical influence; Egyptian thought contributed methods and practical mathematics rather than cosmological frameworks.
source: Diogenes Laërtius, Lives of the Eminent Philosophers I.27; Herodotus, Histories II.109
